label: PUBLIC_SKILL_AUTHORING_API_CONTRACT_DRIFT
output_md:
## skill_boundary_brief.md
Define `api_contract_drift_check` as a reusable public-safe skill for reviewing redacted API contract drift packets. The skill must separate confirmed evidence from inference, keep runtime inputs out of tracked canon, and avoid encoding any real endpoint names, logs, credentials, or `_workspaces` content.

## skill_package_draft.md
Create `.registry/skills/api_contract_drift_check/skill.yaml` plus a short README. Optionally add `codex/SKILL.md` and public-safe templates/checklists. The skill should guide an agent to inspect `openapi_changed_endpoints`, `generated_type_diff_summary`, `route_handler_touchpoints`, `test_failures`, and `owner_notes`, then produce a bounded remediation plan with clear owner handoff.

## skill_resource_bundle_review.md
Use only synthetic or redacted packet examples in any helper script or template. If a script is included, it must accept generic packet fields and operate without project-specific API names. Public-safe examples should demonstrate two endpoints, one type mismatch, one route-handler note, and one failing test name.

## skill_boundary_review.md
Confirmed evidence stays distinct from assumptions. The skill must warn when claims depend on missing spec text, private incident detail, production logs, or runtime workspace files. It should say what can be verified from the packet, what remains uncertain, and which next checks are needed before remediation.

## skill_install_sync_request.md
Prepare a handoff note that tells the operator to sync the tracked package into the local installed mirror through the normal operating procedure. Do not claim that sync was performed. The note should only reference the tracked package path and the intended install target behavior.

## skill_smoke_check.md
Smoke with a tiny synthetic packet containing two endpoints, one generated type mismatch, one route-handler note, and one failing test name. Pass criteria: the skill reports evidence, assumptions, owner handoff, remediation order, and boundary warnings, while keeping the output public-safe.

## skill_release_review.md
Release is acceptable if the package remains reusable, public-safe, and bounded to packet analysis and remediation planning. Reject any version that copies private runtime truth into canon, hardcodes customer endpoints, or implies it performed installed mirror sync.
